revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kabura River]][[ja:鏑川]] [[co:commons:Category:Kabura River]][[ja:鏑川]] [[co:commons:Category:Kabura River]][[ja:鏑川]] [[ja:鏑川]] {{Infobox river |name=Kabura River |image=[[File:Kabura River from Torazu Bridge.jpg|300px|The Kabura River flows along the border of Tomioka City and Shimonita Town, Gunma Prefecture (view downstream from Torazu Bridge)]] |image_caption=The Kabura River flows along the border of Tomioka City and Shimonita Town, Gunma Prefecture
(view downstream from Torazu Bridge) |basin_countries={{JPN}} |watershed=[[Tonegawa River System]] |classification=[[First-class river]] |length=58.8 km |source_elevation=1,375 m |discharge=-- |watershed_area=632.4 km² |source=Mt. Monomi ({{ill|Shimonita, Gunma|en|Shimonita, Gunma|qid=Q1346871|lt=Shimonita|1=Shimonita, Gunma}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}) |mouth={{ill|Karasu River (Gunma)|en|Karasu River (Gunma)|qid=Q3915532|lt=Karasugawa River (Tonegawa System)|1=Karasu River (Gunma)}} (Akutsu Town, {{ill|Takasaki|en|Takasaki|qid=Q336438|lt=Takasaki|1=Takasaki}}) |basin=[[Gunma Prefecture|群馬県]]・[[Nagano Prefecture|長野県]] |footnotes= |sources= }} [[File:Torazu Gorge.jpg|thumb|Torazu Gorge (Tomioka City and Shimonita Town)]] '''Kabura River''' (鏑川, ''Kaburagawa'') is a [[first-class river]] of the [[Tonegawa River System]] flowing through {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan. The river encompasses most of the {{ill|Kanra District, Gunma|en|Kanra District, Gunma|qid=Q1076986|lt=Kanra District|1=Kanra District, Gunma}}, including the Tomioka area. Upstream of its confluence with the {{ill|Nammoku River|ja|南牧川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nammoku River|1=Nanmoku River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21654012}} in Shimonita Town, the river is also known as the "Nishimaki River." == Geography == The river originates near the {{ill|Uchiyama Pass (Gunma-Nagano)|ja|内山峠 (群馬県・長野県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Pass (Gunma-Nagano)|1=Q11393962|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11393962}}, located at the border of {{ill|Shimonita, Gunma|en|Shimonita, Gunma|qid=Q1346871|lt=Shimonita|1=Shimonita, Gunma}}, Gunma Prefecture, and {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|qid=Q495821|lt=Saku|1=Saku, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture. It flows eastward parallel to National Route 254{{Cite web|lang=ja|url=https://www.ktr.mlit.go.jp/ktr_content/content/000802520.pdf|title=Experience and Feel Karasugawa, Kannagawa, Kaburagawa, and Usuigawa Rivers|publisher=Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism Kanto Regional Development Bureau Takasaki River and Road Office|date=|accessdate=December 24, 2024}}. After joining the Nammoku River near the Shimonita town center, it traverses the Tomioka Basin before merging with the {{ill|Karasu River (Gunma)|en|Karasu River (Gunma)|qid=Q3915532|lt=Karasugawa River (Tonegawa System)|1=Karasu River (Gunma)}} near Akutsu Town, Takasaki City. The Shimonita section of the Kabura River features a well-developed [[:en:river terrace|river terrace]] system. These terraces, along with foothill slopes, are extensively utilized for {{ill|Amorphophallus konjac|en|Amorphophallus konjac|qid=Q674443|lt=konjac|1=Amorphophallus konjac}} cultivation. The {{ill|Jōshin Electric Railway Line|ja|上信電鉄線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōshin Electric Railway Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is constructed parallel to much of the river. The upstream basin of the Kabura River, including tributaries like Masaka River and Kumakura River, extends into the Saku City region of Nagano Prefecture. == Municipalities in the Basin == ; Gunma Prefecture : {{ill|Shimonita, Gunma|en|Shimonita, Gunma|qid=Q1346871|lt=Shimonita|1=Shimonita, Gunma}}, {{ill|Nammoku|ja|南牧村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nammoku|1=Q3616320|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3616320}}, {{ill|Tomioka, Gunma|en|Tomioka, Gunma|qid=Q873835|lt=Tomioka|1=Tomioka, Gunma}}, {{ill|Kanra, Gunma|en|Kanra, Gunma|qid=Q1203523|lt=Kanra|1=Kanra, Gunma}}, {{ill|Takasaki|en|Takasaki|qid=Q336438|lt=Takasaki|1=Takasaki}}, {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|qid=Q596486|lt=Fujioka|1=Fujioka, Gunma}} ; Nagano Prefecture : {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|qid=Q495821|lt=Saku|1=Saku, Nagano}} == Tributaries == * Ichinokaya River * {{ill|Osaka River (Tonegawa System)|ja|小坂川 (利根川水系)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka River (Tonegawa System)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nammoku River|ja|南牧川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nammoku River|1=Nanmoku River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21654012}} * Yokose River * Nogami River * {{ill|Ogawa (Gunma)|ja|雄川 (群馬県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogawa (Gunma)|1=Ogawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11659214}} * Takada River * Onzawa River * Hoshikawa River * Amabiki River * Osawa River * {{ill|Yada River (Gunma)|ja|矢田川 (群馬県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada River (Gunma)|1=Yata River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21655004}} * Doai River * {{ill|Ayu River (Gunma)|ja|鮎川 (群馬県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ayu River (Gunma)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == River Facilities == * {{ill|Dochiragawa Dam|ja|道平川ダム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dochiragawa Dam|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Oonita Dam|ja|大仁田ダム|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oonita Dam|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nammoku Headworks|ja|南牧頭首工|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nammoku Headworks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Shimonita Headworks|ja|下仁田頭首工|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimonita Headworks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Nakamura Weir|ja|中村堰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakamura Weir|1=Q28682315|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28682315}} == Bridges == [[File:Joshin 503-504.jpg|thumb|A Jōshin Electric Railway train crossing the Kabura River
(between {{ill|Maniwa Station|en|Maniwa Station|qid=Q11667961|lt=Maniwa Station|1=Maniwa Station}} and {{ill|Yoshii Station (Gunma)|en|Yoshii Station (Gunma)|qid=Q11412619|lt=Yoshii Station (Gunma)|1=Yoshii Station (Gunma)}})]] {| class="wikitable" |- | valign=top| '''U11er Reaches''' * Morihira Bridge * Ōdaira Bridge * Shimosaka Bridge * Morisawa Bridge * Makiguchi Bridge * Tōbu Bridge * Ishibuchi Bridge (National Route 254) | valign=top| '''Middle Reaches''' * Torazu Bridge * Tadakawa Bridge (National Route 254) * Hisari Bridge (National Route 254) * Wago Bridge * Kiribuchi Bridge * Kaburi Bridge ({{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 46|ja|群馬県道46号富岡神流線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Route 46|1=Gunma Prefectural Road Route 46|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11609852}}) * Shin-Kaburi Bridge * Tomioka Bridge (National Route 254) * ({{ill|Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|en|Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line|qid=Q846501|lt=Jōshin Electric Railway Line|1=Jōshin Dentetsu Jōshin Line}}) * Shin-Tomioka Bridge (National Route 254 Bypass) | valign=top| '''Lower Reaches''' * Shiohata Bridge * Yoshii Bridge * Iwasaki Bridge * Tago Bridge ({{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 71|ja|群馬県道・埼玉県道71号高崎神流秩父線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Route 71|1=Gunma Prefectural Road and Saitama Prefectural Road Route 71|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11609885}}) * (Jōshin Electric Railway Line) * Irino Bridge * Matsunokise Bridge * Kaburagawa Bridge ({{ill|Gunma Prefectural Route 30|ja|群馬県道30号寺尾藤岡線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefectural Route 30|1=Gunma Prefectural Road Route 30|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11609806}}) * Shin-Kaburagawa Bridge (Terao-Fujioka Line Bypass) * ({{ill|Jōetsu Shinkansen|en|Jōetsu Shinkansen|qid=Q912219|lt=Jōetsu Shinkansen|1=Jōetsu Shinkansen}}) |- |} == References == {{Reflist}} {{Commonscat|Kaburagawa (Tributary of Karasugawa)}} {{river-stub|pref=Gunma Prefecture|pref2=Nagano Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|鏑川|version=103056596|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鏑川]] {{Commons category|Kabura River}} {{wikidata link|Q11651196}} [[Category:Rivers of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Rivers of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Tone River system]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
